rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The administrator's amended petition is not procedurally ready to be heard; she has not yet addressed the myriad issues that were posted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 5/10/23. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ERIC P. JENSEN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following procedural issues have not been cleared: 1. Proposed Order must be submitted before the hearing. 2. Acceptance of trusteeship from the current trustee (Crystal Elledge) of the Eric Peter Jensen Trust that is the sole devisee under the terms of the trust. PC 10954(b)(4). 3. Petition was to amend this petition to a two-year accounting as the court instructed at the 4/12/23 hearing (LRSF 14.34.C); however, no activity since the hearing. LRSF 14.34.C. 4. Clarification if the following two trusts are the same trust or not: a. The Eric Peter Jensen Trust U/D/T/D 8/8/2000 b. The Eric Peter Jensen Trust FBO Rachel Lenore Jensen U/D/T/D 8/8/2000 PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAUREEN E. CROWE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/20/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The more recent I&A (which should have been captioned "Corrected Partial No. One") includes assets that must be appraised by a probate referee. (If there are no other assets of this estate, the I&A should have been captioned "Corrected Partial No. One / Final".) (2) A personal representative's account must begin with the date of death of the decedent. [LRSF 14.34.] (3) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (4) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (5) Request for Special Notice was filed on 7/7/21 by Amy Martinez, RMO LLP, which does not appear to have been withdrawn yet. (6) Original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (7) A personal representative is responsible for preparing her own accounting. Fees paid to another to prepare the estate accounting must be deducted from the statutory compensation. (8) Detailed schedules must include four columns, showing the date, the source or payee, the description, and the amount. (9) Is the executor requesting additional time to administer the estate? This was not in the prayer or reflected in the petition caption. [CRC 7.102.] This first accounting petition does not include all of the necessary disclosures about the status of the estate. {LRSF 14.35 J.] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/8/23. If the Probate Referee is unable to appraise the non-cash assets in time for an amended accounting and petition to be filed and in time to give notice of the 12/20/23 hearing, Counsel should contact the examiner to request more time. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the investment of cash in excess of the FDIC limit at just one financial institution is merely allowed and not approved. (When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders.)
